title: orobanche alba subsp. cuprea
intro: Orobanche alba subsp. cuprea, also known as White Broomrape, is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Orobanche alba subsp. cuprea include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
